steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the text. The passage focuses on Hannah Ditzler Alspaugh’s scrapbook, concluding that historians see it as both a personal life record and a historical record of nineteenth-century textiles and fashion. Any correct answer must capture that dual significance and keep Alspaugh as the subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Alspaugh inspired other women to save pieces of fabric in scrapbooks and provide historical records of nineteenth-century fashions in the United States.: the passage never says Alspaugh inspired anyone else; this detail is invented and unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — Historians rely on fabric scrapbooks to understand how fashions changed throughout the nineteenth-century United States.: overgeneralizes to scrapbooks in general rather than Alspaugh’s specific work, and no such general claim is made in the text. ✗
  • Choice C — Fabric scrapbooks were a popular hobby for many women in the nineteenth-century United States.: a background detail stated in the opening sentence, not the main idea; it omits Alspaugh entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — Alspaugh's scrapbook provides a detailed account of her life and historical record of fashion trends in the nineteenth-century United States.: captures both roles historians identify — personal life document and historical fashion record — with Alspaugh as the subject. ✓
That matches Choice D — Alspaugh's scrapbook provides a detailed account of her life and historical record of fashion trends in the nineteenth-century United States..
